purpose: Claude Code skill definition for `project-workflow`. It describes trigger conditions, workflow, constraints, and referenced protocol files for that skill. Key content: --- name: project-workflow description: > Run Synapse project setup, discovery, auditing, and analysis workflows. Handles project configuration, onboarding, code review, health checks, TOC management, scaffolding, implementation planning, PKI management, and instrumentation. Use when the user invokes !project, !initialize, !onboard, !context, !review, !health, !scaffold, !plan, !scope, !trace, !contracts, !env_check, !toc, !toc_generate, !toc_upd The file is 75 lines long and should be treated as a knowledge/control-plane source rather than application code.
patterns: Skill-definition pattern: trigger guidance first, then workflow steps and referenced protocol files; these files are loaded on demand by Claude Code.
Uses markdown tables for compact command, file, or option matrices.
conventions: Markdown headings use a single H1 followed by H2/H3 sections for navigable documentation.
Agent-control documents use `{tracker_root}`, `{project_root}`, and `{dashboardId}` placeholders instead of machine-specific paths.
ASCII double hyphen is used in prose where many docs avoid Unicode punctuation for terminal readability.
